questions.question: State the electron configuration of a sodium atom (Z = 11) in s, p, d notation.
Write the full electron configurations of the chlorine atom (Z = 17) and the chloride ion (Cl⁻). State what change in electron number occurs when the Cl⁻ ion forms from a Cl atom.

State the Aufbau principle and use it to write the full electron configuration of calcium (Z = 20). Explain why the 4s subshell is filled before the 3d subshell, referring to relative energies of the subshells.
State the full electron configuration of the Fe²⁺ ion. [Z(Fe) = 26]
Describe Hund's rule and illustrate it using an 'arrows-in-boxes' diagram for the 2p subshell of a carbon atom (Z = 6), which has two electrons in that subshell.

Write the full electron configurations of titanium (Z = 22) and the Ti²⁺ ion. Explain, with reference to energy levels, why the 4s electrons are removed in preference to the 3d electrons when Ti forms Ti²⁺.
State which electrons are lost first when a transition metal atom forms a positive ion.
Write the electron configurations of copper (Z = 29) and the Cu²⁺ ion. State which subshell the electrons are removed from when Cu²⁺ is formed.
Explain why the electron configuration of chromium (Z = 24) is [Ar] 3d⁵ 4s¹ rather than the expected [Ar] 3d⁴ 4s². In your answer, refer to the stability associated with a half-filled d subshell.
State the electron configuration of the oxide ion (O²⁻). [Z(O) = 8]
Write the full electron configurations of manganese (Z = 25) and the Mn²⁺ ion. State clearly which electrons are removed when Mn forms Mn²⁺.

Write the electron configuration of vanadium (Z = 23) and of the V³⁺ ion. Use these configurations to explain which electrons are removed first when V³⁺ is formed, and state the rule that governs the order of electron removal from transition metal ions.
State the two elements in Period 4 (up to Z = 36) that have anomalous electron configurations due to the extra stability of a full or half-filled d subshell.
Write the electron configurations of the Ni²⁺ ion and the Co³⁺ ion. [Z(Ni) = 28; Z(Co) = 27]

A student writes the electron configuration of iron (Z = 26) as 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶ 3d⁸. Identify two errors in this configuration. Write the correct configuration and state which filling rule(s) the student has violated.
